ops.title: Weekly watcher lane crashes on OverlongEvalRows (2 eval rows > max_seq_len=4096) — fix before Sunday 07-26
ops.description: 2026-07-19 04:00 PDT weekly lane: gate lane healthy (challenger correctly rejected by eval-what-you-serve, incumbent v002-20260716 stays), but the watcher lane crashed pre-spend: athome.train.tinker fit() raises OverlongEvalRows(2, 4096) at eval-row validation (before Reservation/any billable call — $0 spent). Nightly accrual added 2 val rows exceeding the recipe's max_tokens=4096. athome drops overlong TRAIN rows with a count but refuses overlong EVAL rows by design. Since T3.1, promotion is gated on the frozen 628-row frame scored locally through the served artifact — the Tinker eval_rows only drive per-checkpoint selection (tinker_val_auc is informational). Recommended shape (verify in code before committing to it): filter overlong rows out of the eval_rows construction in cc_steer/retrain/watcher.py with a loudly-journaled drop count, mirroring athome's train-row precedent; do NOT truncate (silent content change) and do NOT bump max_seq_len without a cost/memory decision. Fix must ship in a PyPI release before Sunday 07-26 04:00 (the lane runs the pinned wheel) — can ride along with the next planned release.

ops.text: overlong eval rows filtered with journaled drop count (or an explicitly decided alternative), test pinning the behavior
released to PyPI + a weekly-lane-shaped invocation passes eval-row validation before Sunday 07-26 04:00
